author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

ccel.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

title: Psalm XCV

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I could wish, brethren, that we were rather listening to our father: but even this is a good thing, to obey our father.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Since therefore he who deigneth to pray for us, hath ordered us, I will speak unto you, beloved, what from the present Psalm Jesus Christ our common Lord shall deign to give us.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now the title of the Psalm is “David’s Song of praise.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The “Song of praise” signifieth both cheerfulness, in that it is a song; and devotion, for it is praise.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For what ought a man to praise more than that which pleaseth him so, that it is impossible that it can displease him?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In the praising of God therefore we praise with security.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There he who praiseth is safe, where he feareth not lest he be ashamed for the object of his praise.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let us therefore both praise and sing; that is, let us praise with cheerfulness and joy.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But what we are about to praise, this Psalm in the following verses showeth us.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “O come, let us sing unto the Lord” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He calleth us to a great banquet of joy, not one of this world, but in the Lord.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For if there were not in this life a wicked joy which is to be distinguished from a righteous joy, it would be enough to say, “Come, let us rejoice;” but he has briefly distinguished it.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is it to rejoice aright?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To rejoice in the Lord.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou shouldest piously joy in the Lord, if thou dost wish safely to trample upon the world.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But what is the word, “Come”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whence doth He call them to come, with whom he wisheth to rejoice in the Lord; except that, while they are afar, they may by coming draw nearer, by drawing nearer they may approach, and by approaching rejoice?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But whence are they afar?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Can a man be locally distant from Him who is everywhere?…It is not by place, but by being unlike Him, that a man is afar from God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is to be unlike Him? it meaneth, a bad life, bad habits; for if by good habits we approach God, by bad habits we recede from God.…If therefore by unlikeness we recede from God, by likeness we approach unto God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What likeness?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That after which we were created, which by sinning we had corrupted in ourselves, which we have received again through the remission of sins, which is renewed in us in the mind within, that it may be engraved a second time as if on coin, that is, the image of our God upon our soul, and that we may return to His treasures.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Let us make a joyful noise unto God, our salvation.”…Consider, beloved, those who make a joyful noise in any ordinary songs, as in a sort of competition of worldly joy; and ye see them while reciting the written lines bursting forth with a joy, that the tongue sufficeth not to express the measure of; how they shout, indicating by that utterance the feeling of the mind, which cannot in words express what is conceived in the heart.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If they then in earthly joy make a joyful noise; might we not do so from heavenly joy, which truly we cannot express in words?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Let us prevent His face by confession” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Confession hath a double meaning in Scripture.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There is a confession of him who praiseth, there is that of him who groaneth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The confession of praise pertaineth to the honour of Him who is praised: the confession of groaning to the repentance of him who confesseth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For men confess when they praise God: they confess when they accuse themselves; and the tongue hath no more worthy use.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Truly, I believe these to be the very vows, of which he speaketh in another Psalm: “I will pay Thee my vows, which I distinguished with my lips.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Nothing is more elevated than that distinguishing, nothing is so necessary both to understand and to do.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How then dost thou distinguish the vows which thou payest unto God?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: By praising Him, by accusing thyself; because it is His mercy, to forgive us our sins.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For if He chose to deal with us after our deserts, He would find cause only to condemn.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “O come,” he said therefore, that we may at last go back from our sins, and that He may not cast up with us our accounts for the past; but that as it were a new account may be commenced, all the bonds of our debts having been burnt.…The more therefore thou despairedst of thyself on account of thy iniquities, do thou confess thy sins; for so much greater is the praise of Him who forgiveth, as is the fulness of the penitent’s confession more abundant.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let us not therefore imagine that we have receded from the song of praise, in understanding here that confession by which we acknowledge our transgressions: this is even a part of the song of praise; for when we confess our sins, we praise the glory of God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And make a joyful noise unto Him with Psalms.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We have already said what it is “to make a joyful noise:” the word is repeated, that it may be confirmed by the act: the very repetition is an exhortation.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For we have not forgotten, so as to wish to be again admonished what was said above, that we should make a joyful noise: but usually in passages of strong feeling a well-known word is repeated, not to make it more familiar, but that the very repetition may strengthen the impression made: for it is repeated that we may understand the feeling of the speaker.…Hear now: “For the Lord is a great God, and a great King above all gods” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 3) “For the Lord will not cast off His people.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Praise be unto Him, and shouts of joy be unto Him!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What people shall He not cast off? we have no right to make our own explanation here: for the Apostle hath prescribed this unto us, he hath explained whereof it is said.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For this was the Jewish people, the people where were the prophets, the people where were the patriarchs, the people begotten according to the flesh from the seed of Abraham; the people in which all the mysteries which promised our Saviour preceded us; the people among whom was instituted the temple, the anointing, the Priest for a figure, that when all these shadows were past, the Light itself might come; this therefore was the people of God; to it were the prophets sent, in it those who were sent were born; to it were delivered and entrusted the revelations of God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What then? is the whole of that people condemned? far be it.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is called the good olive-tree by the Apostle, for it commenced with the patriarchs.…This then is the tree itself: though some of its boughs have been broken, yet all have not.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For if all the boughs were broken, whence is Peter? whence John? whence Thomas? whence Matthew? whence Andrew? whence are all those Apostles? whence that very Apostle Paul who was speaking to us but now, and by his own fruit bearing witness to the good olive?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Were not all these of that people?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whence also those five hundred brethren to whom our Lord appeared after His resurrection?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whence were so many thousands at the words of Peter (when the Apostles, filled with the Holy Spirit, spoke with the tongues of all nations) converted with such zeal for the honour of God and their own accusation, that they who first shed the Lord’s blood in their rage, learnt how to drink it now that they believed?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And all these five thousand were so converted that they sold their own property, and laid the price of it at the Apostles’ feet.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That which one rich man did not do, when he heard from the Lord’s mouth, and sorrowfully departed from Him, this so many thousands of those men by whose hands Christ had been crucified, did on a sudden.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In proportion as the wound was deeper in their own hearts, with the greater eagerness did they seek for a physician.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Since therefore all these were from thence, the Psalm saith of them, “For the Lord will not cast off His people.”…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What doth the Psalm add?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “In His hand are all the corners of the earth” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 4): we recognise the corner stone: the corner stone is Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There cannot be a corner, unless it hath united in itself two walls: they come from different sides to one corner, but they are not opposed to each other in the corner.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The circumcision cometh from one side: the uncircumcision from the other; in Christ both peoples have met together: because He hath become the stone, of which it is written, “The stone which the builders rejected, hath become the head of the corner.”…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For the sea is His and He made it” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the sea is this world, but God made also the sea: nor can the waves rage save only so far as to the shore, where He hath marked their bounds.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There is therefore no temptation, that hath not received its measure.…“And His hands prepared the dry land.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Be thou the dry land: thirst for the grace of God: that as a sweet shower it may come upon thee, may find in thee fruit.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He alloweth not the waves to cover what He hath sown.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And His hands prepared the dry land.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hence also therefore let us shout unto the Lord.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “O come, let us worship, and fall down to Him; and mourn before the Lord our Maker” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 6).…Perhaps thou art burning with the consciousness of a fault; blot out with tears the flame of thy sin: mourn before the Lord: fearlessly mourn before the Lord, who made thee; for He despiseth not the work of His own hands in thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Think not thou canst be restored by thyself.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: By thyself thou mayest fall off, thou canst not restore thyself: He who made thee restoreth thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Let us mourn before the Lord our Maker:” weep before Him, confess unto Him, prevent His face in confession.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For who art thou who mournest before Him, and confessest unto Him, but one whom He created?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The thing created hath no slight confidence in Him who created it, and that in no indifferent fashion, but according to His own image and likeness.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For He is the Lord our God” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But that we may without fear fall down and kneel before Him, what are we?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “We are the people of His pasture, and the sheep of His hand.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: See how elegantly he hath transposed the order of the words, and as it were not given its own attribute to each word; that we may understand these very same to be the sheep, who are also the people.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He said not, the sheep of His pasture, and the people of His hand; which might be thought more congruous, since the sheep belong to the pasture; but He said, “the people of His pasture.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The people are therefore sheep, since he saith, “the people of His pasture:” the people themselves are sheep.…He praiseth these sheep also in the Song of Solomon, speaking of some perfect ones as the teeth of His Spouse the Holy Church: “Thy teeth are like a flock of sheep that are even shorn, which come up from the washing; whereof every one beareth twins, and there is none barren.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What meaneth, “Thy teeth”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: These by whom thou speakest: for the teeth of the Church are those through whom she speaketh.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Of what sort are thy teeth?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Like a flock of sheep that are shorn.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why, “that are shorn”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because they have laid aside the burdens of the world.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Were not those sheep, of which I was a little before speaking, shorn, whom the bidding of God had shorn, when He saith, “Go and sell that thou hast, and give to the poor; and thou shalt find treasure in heaven: and come and follow Me”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They performed this bidding: shorn they came.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And because those who believe in Christ are baptized, what is there said? “which come up from the washing;” that is, come up from the cleansing.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Whereof every one beareth twins.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Those two commandments, wherefrom hang all the Law and the Prophets.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore, “To-day if ye will hear His voice, harden not your hearts” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: O my people, the people of God!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: God addresses His people: not only the people of His which He shall not cast off, but also all His people.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For He speaketh in the corner stone to each wall: that is, prophecy speaketh in Christ, both to the people of the Jews, and the people of the Gentiles.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For some time ye heard His voice through Moses, and hardened your hearts.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He then, when you hardened your hearts, spoke through a herald; He now speaketh by Himself, let your hearts soften.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He who used to send heralds before Him, hath now deigned to come Himself; He here speaketh by His own mouth, He who used to speak by the mouths of the Prophets.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “As in the provocation, and in the day of temptation in the wilderness, where your fathers proved Me” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let such be no more your fathers: imitate them not.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They were your fathers, but if ye do not imitate them, they shall not be your fathers: yet as ye were born of them, they were your fathers.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And if the heathen who came from the ends of the earth, in the words of Jeremias, “The Gentiles shall come unto Thee from the ends of the earth, and shall say, Surely our forefathers have inherited lies, vanity, and things wherein there is no profit:” if the heathen forsook their idols, to come to the God of Israel; ought Israel whom their own God led from Egypt through the Red Sea, wherein He overwhelmed their pursuing foes; whom He led out into the wilderness, fed with manna, never took His rod from correcting them, never deprived them of the blessings of His mercy; ought they to desert their own God, when the heathen have come unto Him?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “When your fathers tempted Me, proved Me, and saw My works.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Forty years long was I very near unto this generation, and said, It is a people that do always err in their hearts; for they have not known My ways” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The forty years have the same meaning as the word “always.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For that number forty indicates the fulness of ages, as if the ages were perfected in this number.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hence our Lord fasted forty days, forty days He was tempted in the desert, and forty days He was with His disciples after His resurrection.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: On the first forty days He showed us temptation, on the latter forty days consolation: since beyond doubt when we are tempted we are consoled.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For His body, that is, the Church, must needs suffer temptations in this world: but that Comforter, who said, “Lo, I am with you alway, even unto the end of the world,” is not wanting.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For this was I with them forty years, to show such a race of men, which alway provoketh Me, even unto the end of the world: because by those forty years He meant to signify the whole of this world’s duration.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 13. …We began with exulting joy: but this Psalm hath ended with great fear: “Unto whom I sware in My wrath, that they should not enter into My rest” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is a great thing for God to speak: how much greater for Him to swear?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou shouldest fear a man when he sweareth, lest he do somewhat on account of his oath against his will: how much more shouldest thou fear God, when He sweareth, seeing He can swear nought rashly?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He chose the act of swearing for a confirmation.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And by whom doth God swear?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: By Himself: for He hath no greater by whom to swear.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: By Himself He confirmeth His promises: by Himself He confirmeth His threats.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let no man say in his heart, His promise is true; His threat is false: as His promise is true, so is His threat sure.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou oughtest to be equally assured of rest, of happiness, of eternity, of immortality, if thou hast executed His commandments; as of destruction, of the burning of eternal fire, of damnation with the devil, if thou hast despised His commandments.…
